task_completion.description: Format the document title ('U.S. Census Bureau News') with Heading 1 style
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'U.S. Census Bureau News' at the top of the document is visibly changed to Heading 1 style (typically larger, blue font in Word 2010). The 'Heading 1' option in the Styles gallery on the Home ribbon may be highlighted when the cursor is on this text.

task_completion.description: Apply Heading 2 style to the first three section headers: 'Overview', 'Income', and 'Poverty'
task_completion.visual_evidence: These three headers are visibly changed to Heading 2 style (typically blue font, slightly smaller than Heading 1). The 'Heading 2' option in the Styles gallery is used.

task_completion.description: Apply Heading 2 style to the remaining section headers: 'Supplemental Poverty Measure', 'Health Insurance Coverage', and 'Contact Information'
task_completion.visual_evidence: These three headers are visibly changed to Heading 2 style. The agent will likely need to scroll down through the document to find and format them.

task_completion.description: Ensure body paragraphs use Normal style
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text paragraphs containing the statistics and information remain in the default Normal style (typically black, smaller font) and are not accidentally formatted as headings.

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the document
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the Save icon (floppy disk) in the Quick Access Toolbar at the top left, or uses the File > Save menu.

integrity.description: The agent used the Microsoft Word 2010 interface to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: The formatting is done by clicking elements in the Word ribbon, not by running Python scripts or terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent used the actual Styles gallery to apply headings, rather than manually changing font size and color
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent clicks on 'Heading 1' and 'Heading 2' in the Styles section of the Home tab, rather than using the Font size dropdown or Font color button to mimic the appearance of headings.

integrity.description: The agent modified the provided census press release document
integrity.visual_evidence: The document content matches the US Census Bureau press release about income and poverty statistics, and the Word window title shows 'census_press_release.docx'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses Microsoft Word 2010. The document 'census_press_release.docx' contains a synthetic US Census Bureau press release. The title 'U.S. Census Bureau News' must be formatted as Heading 1. Six specific section headers must be formatted as Heading 2: 'Overview', 'Income', 'Poverty', 'Supplemental Poverty Measure', 'Health Insurance Coverage', and 'Contact Information'. All other text (35 paragraphs) must remain in Normal style. The document must be saved after formatting.
